Compares PBT approach against OtterTune (GP+Lasso), CDBTune (DDPG RL), LlamaTune (BO+random embeddings), and GPTuner (LLM-guided BO), highlighting PBT advantages in cold-start, stability, parallelism, and reproducibility.

properties.description: Future work document proposing population-level workload fingerprinting, cross-workload warm-starting via population archive, and knob importance transfer for PBT.
Setup instructions for .env database credentials, Python dependencies (psycopg2, psutil, numpy), and sysbench 1.1.0 source build across platforms.

Canonical workflow for reproducing default-vs-tuned evaluations including Docker-isolated and bare-metal modes, seed handling, statistical endpoint policy (Wilcoxon, Holm correction), and reproducibility checklist.

Describes the scoring-v2 pipeline: WorkloadFeatures extraction, QuantileUtilityNormalizer, FeatureDrivenWeightModel (floor-constrained softmax), CompositeScorer with reliability gate, and migration from fixed_v1.

Documents the fractional transfer system for cross-hardware portability, resource detection layer, dynamic knob specifications for 13 hardware-dependent knobs, aggregate memory budget validation, and warm-starting workflow.

Theoretical foundation for the multi-objective scoring metric: latency (P95/P99), throughput (TPS/QphH), memory efficiency as resource regularization, error penalty as death constraint, and scoring policy versioning.
Comprehensive inclusion/exclusion policy for all 397 PostgreSQL knobs with 17 exclusion reason codes (internal_context, data_integrity, security_transport, stability, etc.) and re-enable criteria.
Documents the SMAC3-based BO baseline runner with Random Forest and GP surrogate facades, Pilot+Freeze normalization strategy, and PBT session parity matching for controlled comparison.
Explains KnobSpace (search space definition, sampling, validation) and KnobApplicator (runtime application with two-layer validation, context manager pattern, and rollback mechanism).

Documents the three core PBT classes: Worker (individual state), Evolution (exploit via truncation selection + explore via perturbation), and Population (orchestrator managing parallel evaluation and convergence).
Documents the pbt_vs_bo_comparison.py script that performs global post-hoc rescoring across PBT and BO runs, generating CSV tables and publication-ready convergence/Pareto/resource-efficiency PDFs.
Documents the Evaluator (workload execution orchestrator), PerformanceMetrics (structured latency/throughput/resource measurements), and scoring system (composite fitness function for PBT).
Implementation summary of PopulationConfig, GenerationResult, and Population class with parallel/sequential evaluation, exploit-explore triggering, convergence detection, and early stopping.
Describes the four-module system (config, database, knobs, scripts) for PostgreSQL connection management and knob metadata retrieval with type safety and centralized configuration.
Comprehensive future work guide for adding MySQL as second DBMS via Database Adapter Pattern, covering 11 implementation phases (R1-R11), ~35-50 file changes, and ~100+ MySQL system variables.
Architecture decision record for explicit Sysbench workload mode handling (oltp_read_only, oltp_read_write, oltp_write_only) with session metadata, CLI contracts, and output path partitioning.

Architecture decision record adopting feature-driven composite scoring with workload feature vectors, policy-controlled weight floors, robust normalization, and reliability gate shared across tuning/rescoring/evaluation.
Common interface (prepare + validate + execute) implemented by all benchmark executors, enabling PostgresInstanceManager to initialize schemas without benchmark-specific knowledge.
Stores hardware-relative knobs as fractions of detected resource limits (e.g., shared_buffers = 0.25 of RAM), enabling cross-hardware config portability without OOM crashes.
BO-specific strategy: pilot phase collects diverse metrics with Sobol design, then freezes normalization bounds to prevent surrogate model training signal corruption.
Multiplicative gate G in [0,1] applied before score aggregation to prevent unstable or failed runs from winning ranking. Fatal failures set G=0, error rates decay linearly.
Maps raw performance metrics to [0,1] using robust quantile anchors (5th/95th percentile) with drift detection, recalibration, and saturation handling.
Converts workload features to metric weights via floor-constrained softmax over feature-conditioned logits with temperature scaling.
Proposed approach to characterize workloads using distributional statistics aggregated across all workers' runtime metrics, providing richer signatures than single-configuration observations.
Proposed store of final-generation populations tagged with workload fingerprints and hardware metadata for nearest-neighbor cross-workload warm-starting.
Proposed strategy using fANOVA/SHAP-derived importance rankings from prior sessions to focus perturbation on impactful knobs for similar workload classes.
Risk of performance degradation when warm-starting from dissimilar workloads, mitigated by similarity thresholding, diversity preservation, and PBT's exploit-explore recovery.
Abstract interface pattern proposed for multi-DBMS support, allowing PBT core algorithm and scoring to remain DBMS-agnostic while encapsulating PostgreSQL/MySQL-specific operations.
Safety mechanism that proportionally scales down memory knobs (shared_buffers + max_connections*work_mem + maintenance_work_mem) when their sum exceeds 80% of available RAM.
Classification system with 17 exclusion categories for 397 PostgreSQL knobs, distinguishing absolute exclusions (data_integrity, internal_context) from conditional ones (stability, os_alignment).
Sequential Model-based Algorithm Configuration framework used as BO baseline, supporting Random Forest (HyperparameterOptimizationFacade) and GP (BlackBoxFacade) surrogates with Sobol initial design.
Technique to fairly compare PBT and BO by pooling all raw metrics, recalibrating normalization ranges once globally, then assigning comparable GlobalScore values.

nodes.label: PVLDB LaTeX Template (acmart.cls)
nodes.rationale: PVLDB requires the acmart document class and ACM-Reference-Format bibliography style; these are external dependencies that must be placed alongside main.tex for the build to succeed.

nodes.label: latexmk Build Flow
nodes.rationale: Using latexmk as the build driver ensures reproducible multi-pass compilation (pdflatex + bibtex) and is the de-facto standard for academic LaTeX projects.

nodes.label: Modular Section Layout
nodes.rationale: Each major section (introduction, background, methodology, evaluation, conclusion) is kept in its own file under sections/ to enable parallel authoring without merge conflicts.

nodes.label: fANOVA Variance Decomposition
nodes.rationale: fANOVA provides mathematically precise marginal importance and pairwise interaction scores for PostgreSQL knobs. It uses a C++ random forest via pyrfr/SWIG, which constrains the environment to Python 3.9.

nodes.label: pyrfr (C++ Random Forest wrapper)
nodes.rationale: pyrfr is a SWIG-based C++ wrapper for random forests required by fANOVA. Its strict SWIG/C-API bindings crash on Python 3.10+, forcing a Python 3.9 constraint for the analysis environment.

nodes.label: TPC-H Benchmark
nodes.rationale: TPC-H is the standard OLAP decision-support benchmark. The 8-table star/snowflake schema and 22-query power test provide a well-understood workload for evaluating PostgreSQL knob tuning under analytical query patterns.

nodes.label: ConfigSpace (hyperparameter search space)
nodes.rationale: ConfigSpace defines the hyperparameter search space that fANOVA operates on. The analysis importance module maps loaded tuning data onto ConfigSpace to compute variance decomposition.

nodes.label: OtterTune (SIGMOD 2017)
nodes.author: Van Aken, Pavlo, Gordon, Zhang
nodes.rationale: Automatic database management system tuning via Gaussian Process regression and Lasso feature selection. Proposes workload mapping to transfer prior tuning sessions.

nodes.label: OtterTune Full Paper (SIGMOD 2017)
nodes.author: Van Aken, Pavlo, Gordon, Zhang
nodes.rationale: Full SIGMOD proceedings version of OtterTune with extended evaluation on MySQL and PostgreSQL. Introduces factor analysis for dimensionality reduction and workload characterization via internal runtime metrics.

nodes.label: CDBTune (SIGMOD 2019)
nodes.author: Zhang, Liu, Zhou, Li, Xiao
nodes.rationale: End-to-end deep reinforcement learning (DDPG) for cloud database configuration tuning. Eliminates need for prior workload data and manual feature engineering.

nodes.label: CDBTune+ (2021)
nodes.author: Zhang, Liu, Zhou, Li, Xiao
nodes.rationale: Extended version of CDBTune with improvements to sample efficiency and reward shaping. Adds query-level knob sensitivity analysis and adaptive action space.

nodes.label: QTune (VLDB 2019)
nodes.author: Li, Zhou, Li, Zhang
nodes.rationale: Query-aware database tuning using deep RL. Encodes individual SQL queries via a tree-structured LSTM to produce query-specific configuration recommendations across three granularities: query-level, workload-level, and cluster-level.

nodes.label: LlamaTune (VLDB 2022)
nodes.author: Kanellis, Ding, Kroth, Müller, Joshi
nodes.rationale: Sample-efficient DBMS configuration tuning via structured random embeddings that reduce high-dimensional knob spaces to low-dimensional subspaces. Uses Bayesian Optimization (SMAC) in the projected space with biased sampling to respect knob importance.

nodes.label: GPTuner (VLDB 2024)
nodes.author: Lao, Wang, Li, Li, Chen, Zhang
nodes.rationale: Database tuning using LLM-guided search space pruning followed by Bayesian Optimization. Uses GPT-4 to interpret PostgreSQL manual descriptions for knob importance ranking, constraint extraction, and initial value suggestion. Employs SMAC with coarse-to-fine optimization.

nodes.label: DBTune (2024)
nodes.rationale: Database tuning system combining multiple optimization strategies including knowledge transfer and adaptive search space management.

nodes.label: PBTune (PVLDB Draft)
nodes.rationale: The user's own paper. Applies Population-Based Training to PostgreSQL configuration tuning with parallel worker evaluation, feature-driven scoring, tiered knob spaces, and adaptive metric normalization.

nodes.label: Gaussian Process Regression
nodes.rationale: Surrogate model used by OtterTune to predict optimal knob configurations from prior observations.

nodes.label: Lasso Feature Selection
nodes.rationale: Used by OtterTune for automatic knob importance ranking via L1 regularization.

nodes.label: Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG)
nodes.rationale: Actor-critic deep RL algorithm used by CDBTune for continuous knob space optimization. Handles mixed discrete/continuous action spaces.

nodes.label: Workload Mapping
nodes.rationale: OtterTune technique that maps a new workload to the most similar previously-tuned workload using internal runtime metrics, enabling transfer of tuning knowledge.

nodes.label: Query Encoding via Tree-LSTM
nodes.rationale: QTune encodes SQL parse trees into fixed-length vectors using tree-structured LSTMs for query-aware tuning at query, workload, and cluster granularity.

nodes.label: Structured Random Embeddings
nodes.rationale: LlamaTune projects high-dimensional knob spaces into low-dimensional subspaces via random linear embeddings with biased sampling, dramatically reducing the number of BO iterations needed.

nodes.label: LLM-Guided Search Space Pruning
nodes.rationale: GPTuner uses GPT-4 to read PostgreSQL documentation and generate structured knowledge about knob importance, valid ranges, constraints, and dependencies — pruning the search space before BO.

nodes.label: Population-Based Training
nodes.rationale: PBTune's core technique: parallel population of workers with exploit (copy best) and explore (perturb) steps, combining strengths of random search and hand-tuning.

nodes.label: Bayesian Optimization (BO)
nodes.rationale: Sequential model-based optimization used by OtterTune (GP), LlamaTune (SMAC/RF), GPTuner (SMAC coarse-to-fine), and this project's BO baseline.

nodes.label: TPC-C Benchmark
nodes.rationale: OLTP benchmark used for evaluation by OtterTune, CDBTune, and QTune.

nodes.label: TPC-H Benchmark (Papers)
nodes.rationale: OLAP decision-support benchmark used by GPTuner, LlamaTune, and PBTune for evaluation.

nodes.label: Sysbench (Papers)
nodes.rationale: OLTP micro-benchmark used by CDBTune, LlamaTune, GPTuner, and PBTune for workload evaluation.

nodes.label: YCSB Benchmark
nodes.rationale: Yahoo Cloud Serving Benchmark used by LlamaTune evaluation.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL (Tuning Target)
nodes.rationale: Primary DBMS target for OtterTune, GPTuner, LlamaTune, and PBTune.

nodes.label: MySQL (Tuning Target)
nodes.rationale: DBMS target for OtterTune and CDBTune evaluation.

nodes.label: Factor Analysis for Knob Pruning

nodes.label: Reward Shaping for DB Tuning
nodes.rationale: CDBTune defines reward as the change in throughput/latency relative to the initial default configuration, avoiding sparse rewards.

nodes.label: Coarse-to-Fine BO
nodes.rationale: GPTuner applies a two-stage BO: coarse search over the full space followed by fine-grained search around the best region.
